events.label: reasoning
events.text:
We need answer JSON only. Need interpret scenario: field service HVAC-R charging & diagnostics, observed condition gauge readout interpretation (low charge vs TXV starving vs dirty coil - classic FD triple). Work rough-complete. Need estimate/interpret measurable condition and state why out of tolerance. Need decide pass/fail/needs_more_info. Since observed condition is a misinterpretation? The scenario says observed field condition is gauge readout interpretation (low charge vs TXV starving vs dirty coil - classic FD triple). We need judge whether within tolerance. Likely the technician misdiagnoses low charge when actually TXV starving or dirty coil, leading to improper charging. Since work is rough-complete, not closed out, and component condition installed-defective. Need fail because charging based on gauge interpretation without subcooling/superheat and mfr charts is non-compliant/dangerous? Let's parse.

The "classic FD triple" maybe "frost, discharge, ..."? Actually FD triple in HVAC? Low charge vs TXV starving vs dirty coil - all present similar low suction pressure. Need use superheat and subcooling to differentiate. If gauge readout interpretation is wrong, condition out of tolerance. The task: "Estimate or interpret the measurable condition and state why it is out of tolerance." We need provide value numeric reading or computed quantity. We can estimate e.g. suction pressure 55 psig for R-410A corresponds to ~30°F saturation, superheat maybe 35°F (too high) indicating low charge or starving TXV; subcooling low 3°F indicates low charge; if subcooling normal/high and superheat high, TXV starving; if pressures low but subcooling normal and superheat normal, dirty coil/airflow. Since scenario says gauge readout interpretation (low charge vs TXV starving vs dirty coil - classic FD triple), maybe the measurable cue is gauge pressures and temperatures. We need decide which is actual. But no specific numbers given. We can estimate a representative out-of-tolerance condition: low suction pressure with excessive superheat, e.g. suction pressure 55 psig, superheat 35°F, subcooling 2°F, indicating undercharge. Or maybe the issue is misdiagnosis. The required output includes value. We can set value to e.g. 55 (psig suction) or superheat 35. Need choose.

Let's think about what the benchmark expects. It asks: "Estimate or interpret the measurable condition and state why it is out of tolerance." The scenario describes a visible/estimable cue: gauge readout interpretation (low charge vs TXV starving vs dirty coil - classic FD triple). Reference: mfr charging charts. We need apply reference material. The decision likely fail because the condition is out of tolerance (low charge or starving) and work not completed correctly. Risk maybe high because improper charging can damage compressor. Component condition installed-defective. Percent complete maybe 70. Findings: suction pressure/superheat out of manufacturer spec; charging diagnosis not verified with subcooling/superheat and mfr chart. Actions: recover/recharge to OEM specs, check TXV bulb placement and superheat, clean condenser/evap, verify airflow, use charging chart. Rationale: gauge pressure alone cannot distinguish the triple; measured superheat/subcooling deviates from mfr chart, indicating undercharge/TXV issue/dirty coil. References: mfr charging charts, maybe EPA 608, ACCA? But references field should be code or standard anchors. We can include "OEM charging chart", "ANSI/ACCA 5 QI-2015", "EPA Section 608". But maybe just "mfr charging charts".

Need ensure JSON valid. No prose outside. We must write to /app/answer.json. In this environment, final answer should be JSON only. The system says return exactly one valid JSON object. So final should be JSON.

Need fill all fields. Let's decide values.

decision: "fail"
risk: "high"
work_stage: "rough-complete"
component_conditions: ["installed-defective"]
percent_complete: 70
value: 55 (suction pressure psig) maybe also incl…
